description:
Goal: make selective-receive hit semantics explicit and provable before changing compiler2 native codegen.

Output contract:
- sender-side selective-receive probe is the authoritative hit path
- a hit bypasses the mailbox entirely
- a hit copies only the winning arm's projected capture set into the receiver heap
- the receiver resumes from a receiver-owned outcome closure, not from a mailbox message
- a miss deep-copies the full message into the mailbox

Changes:
- update scheduler/receive docs to describe outcome-closure delivery explicitly
- extend runtime selective-receive tests to prove mailbox bypass and projection-only hit behavior
- add one minimal compiler2 native regression that names the intended receive->resume->arithmetic behavior
- use production paths and telemetry where possible

Validation:
- focused runtime selective-receive tests
- focused compiler2 native selective-receive regression
- docs match code

comments.text:
Pinned the selective-receive outcome-closure contract in docs and tests:
- updated scheduler-zero-arg-closures and dispatch-matrix docs to state that sender-side receive hits are outcome-closure delivery, not mailbox delivery
- added runtime proof send_probe_hit_projects_only_matched_payload_into_outcome_closure showing mailbox bypass plus projection-only copy on hit
- added an ignored compiler2 native regression pin for receive->resume->arithmetic in drive_test

Validation so far:
- cargo fmt --all
- cargo test send_probe_
- cargo test compiler2_interp_runs_selective_receive_with_make_ref_from_backend_artifacts

Open cleanup note: drive_test still has the pre-existing ignored native debug helper that belongs to fz-go4.10.4, so landing this ticket will need a partial commit or that follow-on to land first.

title: Define selective-receive outcome-closure contract in docs and tests
